I'm part of this project to leverage my background as a Magic the Gathering judge, a game famous for its ability to use the English language to communicate game mechanics more accurately than most lawyers, to help template the game.
I haven't yet gotten around to actually rewriting cards yet, as I'm still in the research stage, but I hope that when I do get around to it I don't get my semantics nitpicked too much. I'm of course open to constructive criticism but I'll be making the executive decisions on the massive rewrite that I'm attempting to implement.

This is why I initially wrote what I wrote, and will debate with myself and possibly y'all case-by-case when strict adherence to my guidelines don't make perfect sense.
I'm fine with "`(...)each gain(...)`" personally, but again I haven't made it to the point where I make that decision.
Ah yeah, I haven't got to reading back much <:aurita_dizzy:1408324284702654556>

a great flood happens, marking an era change
more stuff happens
the events of the game seem to be between A.F. 315-317

but for clarity I'd personally write Command Card (at least i did for kaiden)

anyways is this too wordy for her innate?
Horla's "Strike" and "Defend" Command Cards are treated as Psalms. When Horla's Command Cards trigger their Leap Effect, she enters the emotion state associated with that Psalm. Horla can only have one emotion state active at a time, and triggering a different Psalm's Leap Effect will change the emotion state to the corresponding emotion. Each emotion has a team-wide passive buff that is active while Horla remains in that emotion. Each emotion state enhances a different effect of Horla's Exalt.\nFor every 1% Death Resistance Horla has, the passive effect value of Horla's current emotion state increases by 0.1%.
looking at horla's cards I think just using emotion and metaphor should work
I wanted to be as clear as possible
emotion itself is being used as a replacement for stance
so saying something like "switch to the Anger emotion stance" would be redundant
yeah
then again on second though saying "switch to the Anger emotion" would kind of be pretty bad at communicating what exactly it does
since if you don't know how horla works beforehand you'd never really consider that a permanent passive stance
I think that can be explained via better tooltipping for how her emotions even work

> are treated as Psalms
Sounds unnecessary
yeah I agree
I think the firs tsentence doesn't need to exist since her Strike and Defend are already renamed
this sounds good to me, the innate textbox scrolls so I feel like wordiness isn't too much of an issue as long as it clearly communicates the effects
one thing I would mention is that emotion states are retained between battles, since that's not immediately clear if you haven't used horla before
yeah on second thought it's explained in her innate so it's fine
yeah that's a good idea
"The emotion state persists throughout the exploration" sounds in line with how they usually word it
I'm starting to get more sold on applied over inflicted btw
it works in more instances even if inflicted sounds better for bleed/poison

I do get where you're coming from but to be fair, horla is pretty much like one of the only characters (maybe the only?) where her strike and defense cards are uniquely changed to interact with her custom mechanic rather than just being standard aliemus gen filler cards

so if you're encountering horla for the first time without context while being used to strike/def from other characters, without that line as part of the innate it's easy to get tripped up by thinking that horla has no strike/def cards because they've been renamed, which would be wrong because snarl/elegy are still considered strike/def cards and thus will interact with relics/mechanics in the corresponding way
so that line does serve a purpose which is basically informing that those cards are still considered basic strike/def cards and will work with any corresponding mechanics
I guess it doesn't really matter if it's there TBH
it's not like there's a limit right
for innates, not that i'm aware of

let's just use Kumu.

content: though not directly linked to anything in morimens it is a vague possibility it could be inspired somehow by this
significantly more likely is the old hebrew word "qum", an imperative meaning to rise or to stand, which shows up often in the bible

https://www.abarim-publications.com/Dictionary/q/q-w-mfin.html
https://bam.sites.uiowa.edu/RTL/qum

embeds.title: RTL Words: QUM (קום)
embeds.description: The word קום (qum, pronounced KOOM) is both a Hebrew and Aramaic verb meaning "to stand, arise." The term can also mean "to rise up (against)" a person in combat. An Aramaic example is found in Daniel 3:24: "Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and rose up (וקם) quickly." A Hebrew example can be

content:
also if we're talking about verbs for gameplay templating, I'm working on a comprehensive guide for those that I'll submit for review hopefully later today.
I sniffed up discussion of the word inflict so I'd like to drop this here to help inform the choices made in that regard (as I'll be changing any terminology-based text lines later down the line regardless)
This is a WORK IN PROGRESS and subject to change:
```GAIN: Any BENEFICIAL combat status that has stacks, Shield included, being increased onto the self (relative).
     Also, Aliemus and Keyflare.
SUFFER: When a DETRIMENTAL status is applied to the self (relative).
INFLICT: When a DETRIMENTAL status is applied to the opponent (relative), by the executor.
CLEANSE: When a DETRIMENTAL status is removed from the self (relative).
DISPEL: When a DETRIMENTAL status is removed from the opponent (relative), by the executor.
GRANT: Aliemus
RECOVER: Health/HP (Recover? Restore? Heal?)```
i'm thinking this is likely it due to the Genesis influences that show up starting from ch7 onwards
basically, I want to call her "Qum", does anyone have any counterarguments against
this is the most likely, absolutely. mad props for digging that up, amazing work.

Sadly though I really still do think that's far too close to Cum for comfort *(I'd like to attempt to avoid any names that ultimately result in that pronunciation)*
-# but that's just, like, my opinion, man
Do the original Chinese word(s) used for the name of the creature in question bear any meaning?
And would the name "Lisa" have any relevant reflections on the meaning of the name ultimately chosen?
nope, the chinese name doesn't seem to have any meaning whatsoever and is used solely for the phonetic aspect
and nope, lisa is also a purely phonetic name
most of the names in chinese are just transliterations of western names phonetically
counterargument: koom is a lot closer to coom and that appears to be pretty commonly used nowadays
particularly in the gacha gaming space since i see people use coomers and coomer game a lot with regards to butt jiggler games and whatnot
case in point as to why we should also avoid anything that results in the pronunciation "coom", you made my point for me <:shrug:1003188954218123314>
yeah but unfortunately that is what the character is called, so not much we can do about it
if we must I'd support Qum

My two suggestions are this:
1. I really liked Kumu when they started using that, it's a name that fits the vibe of the creature. *(Not to mention it's a canid monster, so the general rule of two-syllable names over single-syllable names for the sake of beckoning applies)*
2. If you're dead-set on a single-syllable name, I'd most prefer the spelling of the word Qum that does literally appear in the new testament in Mark 5:41:
`He took her by the hand and said to her, “Talitha koum!” (which means “Little girl, I say to you, get up!”).`
so Koum?
koum could work
well at least that looks like it could be pronounced "Koume"
in a way that rhymes with "cone", yes
(despite koum in this context still being literally pronounced coom)
the extra allowance for cognitive dissonance is welcomed lol

for whatever my opinion is worth, my significant preference is still in localizing to Kumu, and I draw a hard line against anything else beside Koum.
I'm gonna get offline and get started with my workday now lol

content: enos it is
Ah that's good.
Glad it wasn't an obscure reference. Thanks!

Alright, one last response before I log off.
My intention is for Keyflare to use the verb `Accrue` or `Accumulate`, and for all instances of giving Aliemus, I would strictly use one of two terms already in use, depending on context:
`Gain(s)` will be used subjectively, for any effect in which an Awakener gives themselves Aliemus (Strike, Defend, Rouse, some Relics etc)
`Grant(s)` will be used objectively, for any effect **not attributed to the target Awakener** that gives said Awakener Aliemus (Nautila card, Thais card, some Relics, Embryos etc)
Again, I'll publish my finished style guide for review when I'm done with it. <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202> 
See y'all later ✌️

I'm curious why you'd use "*grant [thing] to [target]*" when "*[target] gains [thing]*" makes perfect sense and allows for flexible formulation.
Also, *grant* seems like it wouldn't go well with random targeting, though that's a minor concern.
using separate terms for gain vs grant is also going to run into issues with Thais's card since both her and a target get aliemus
and if you use "Thais gains x aliemus and grants x aliemus to a target awakener" that just gets kinda awkward and wordy

Also worth noting for what it's worth that her two hydra-head boss form summons are named [Azhdaha](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Azhdaha) and [Lernaeas](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lernaean_Hydra)

finally finished all the main story stage detail text...there's less interest in story work than I thought so I'll start working on editing the story - I'll start from ch4 onwards for now since those need the most work

Hi guysss!! Update!

So I just got Light to let me hire even more human professional localizers to work on the English version of Main Story...! 

1) If you've** already submitted** your proofread version of main story stuff: those will be incorporated! Thank you!
2) If you plan to work on the Main Story, but haven't started yet: I recommend **prioritize working on other stuff first**🎉
I was like gib money please
He just said yes
Also in case you find that the human translation we provide in-game later still have rooms for improvement you can always submit issues/suggest improvements later!
oh! okay well back to not working on the main story then
may I ask if the staff will be part of this discord?
we are still on the hiring stage
just wanted to let you guys know so we don't waste your time
I'd like to be able to bounce some questions off them occasionally

speaking of funding, will we as volunteers be compensated for the work we're doing? It was never made explicitly clear, but I was hoping at least to get some Silver for the effort <:shrug:1003188954218123314>
I like that you are speaking my language... and thanks for starting this topic.
<:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541>  : *Silver pwease*

Hi! 

Sorry for the potential confusion caused; the volunteer project itself involves no financial compensation, but we can defintely offer other forms of rewards, in addition to the in-game shout out.
We were supposed to make everyone sign the NDA first, where it would be made more clear; because of circumstances the Docusign links haven't been sent out, but I've just talked to my colleague to speed it up!

also can I ask:

1. Is there a rough estimate of how long the hiring/onboarding process will take? Just a rough order-of-magnitude level of estimate is fine, since I wasn't sure what timescale we were talking about here (2 weeks? 2 months? several months?)

2. How often is it planned for our changes to actually be patched to live servers? Is it only with every major story patch that happens after ~2-3 months, or is it with every version patch that occurs every 28 days? 

I'm mostly just asking so I can figure out how to prioritize - if new hires and onboarding means it would take 3 months before a professional translator starts work but our work is planned to be pushed to live after Kathigu-ra's banner ends in 17 days, then there would still be a few months of gap before the story translation is actually updated to a proper level. In that case, I think I would prefer to just do a first-pass basic editing of the main story to at least make it readable and fix any glaring naming/pronoun inconsistencies, since that shouldn't take me more than a few days of work. Conversely, if you expect to have new hires up and running within 2 weeks and our edits aren't making to live for another month or two, then that would be pointless work so I'll skip it entirely

1. I'm not sure about the hiring time yet, but our goal is to have the Arc1 main story chapters reworked by humans by our 2nd Anni, which is like 3-4 months away. 
2. We're planning to patch changes into live servers along with **every version update.**
I personally would like maybe an in-game profile picture of the Keeper (Both genders ideally!) wearing reading glasses and holding up a little book, maybe in chibi style?

As for how to carry out our proofreading and editing with the news of hiring additional professional localizers, I agree with Lens that touching up on mixed pronouns and inconsistent names in arc 1 makes sense.
Outside of that, I suppose we could still tackle the side-stories? As it seems like those won’t be targeted by the new hires for months still?
Yes!
I just realized they changed Horla's cards to Psalms from Threnodies
lol
Thank you! In that case I'll try to do a first-pass basic editing of the story mode over the next few days since that should at least help for a few months
